description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, commonly found in urban environments for emergency water access. The hydrant features a domed top with a central operating nut, a cylindrical body, and three prominent hose connection outlets—two on the sides and one facing forward. The surface is painted a glossy red, with visible metallic wear along the edges, suggesting heavy-duty cast iron construction. The base is hexagonal and slightly flared, providing stability when mounted to the ground. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant appears robust and well-maintained, suitable for street or sidewalk installation.
description: A glossy red, heavy-duty fire hydrant with three hose outlets and a domed top, showing metallic wear on edges.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central hose outlet, operating nut on top, and symmetrical side outlets.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the right-side outlet and the cylindrical body with visible surface wear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying both side outlets and the smooth, unmarked back surface.
description_view_3: Angled view from the left, showing the left-side outlet and the domed top with central nut.
